source.title: FY 2026 Multifamily Tax Subsidy Project Income Limits Briefing
source.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rules.title: Multifamily Tax Subsidy Project income limits
rules.plain_english: LIHTC projects use HUD-published Multifamily Tax Subsidy Project income limits. The applicable value depends on the project, area, income band, household size, and effective year.

rules.title: 60 percent MTSP income limit
rules.plain_english: For the 40-60 minimum set-aside, the published 60 percent MTSP limit is based on 120 percent of HUD's very-low-income limit. RealDoor uses the published table rather than recomputing the limit.

citations.label: FY 2026 MTSP Briefing, Revenue Ruling 89-24 discussion

rules.title: LIHTC 20-50 minimum set-aside test
rules.plain_english: Under the 20-50 test described in Internal Revenue Code section 42, at least 20 percent of the project's residential units must be rent-restricted and occupied by households at or below 50 percent of area median gross income. This project-level rule is not an individual eligibility decision.

rules.title: LIHTC 40-60 minimum set-aside test
rules.plain_english: Under the 40-60 test described in Internal Revenue Code section 42, at least 40 percent of the project's residential units must be rent-restricted and occupied by households at or below 60 percent of area median gross income. The taxpayer's minimum set-aside election is irrevocable.

rules.title: Rent-restricted unit calculation
rules.plain_english: A residential unit is rent-restricted when its gross rent does not exceed 30 percent of the imputed income limitation applicable to that unit.

rules.title: Utility allowances are included in gross rent
rules.plain_english: For the LIHTC gross-rent definition in the loaded section 42 source, gross rent includes the applicable utility allowance. Certain rental-assistance payments and qualifying supportive-service fees are excluded as described in the statute.

rules.title: Bedroom-based imputed occupancy for rent limits
rules.plain_english: For imputed income limits used to calculate LIHTC rent restrictions, a unit without a separate bedroom is treated as occupied by one person; a unit with bedrooms uses 1.5 persons per bedroom. This rent-limit convention is distinct from the renter's actual household size.

rules.title: Next available unit rule after income increases
rules.plain_english: A unit may continue to be treated as low-income after the occupants' income rises if it remains rent-restricted. When income rises above 140 percent of the applicable limit, the next available comparable-or-smaller unit rule described in section 42 applies.

rules.title: Biweekly wage annualization
rules.plain_english: A confirmed gross amount paid every two weeks is annualized by multiplying it by 26 pay periods. This is a calculation aid; the housing provider determines which income method applies.
